title: Voluntary School Prayer Protection Act of 2025
short_title: Voluntary School Prayer Protection Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill stops the Department of Education from giving money to public schools that don't allow voluntary prayer. The prayer must be the kind that is protected by the Constitution. Schools cannot get federal funding if they have rules that stop students from praying voluntarily or make it hard for them to pray. The bill is meant to protect students' right to pray in school if they choose to.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
policy_area: Civil Rights and Liberties, Minority Issues

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Voluntary School Prayer Protection Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2 Funding Contingent on Respect for Constitutionally Protected School Prayer
sections.text: The federal government will not give money from the Department of Education to any state or local school system that has rules against voluntary prayer in public schools. This applies when the prayer is protected by the Constitution. Schools cannot stop students or others from praying voluntarily if the Constitution protects that prayer. However, no person can be forced to pray or to change what someone else prays about in public schools.
